## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator.dart` (1225 lines) — the only oversized impl file. Concrete split seams:
  - **Content-gating logic** (`_shouldSkipForAwaitingContent`, `setAwaitingContent`, `isAwaitingContent`, `_agentsAwaitingContent`, lines ~144–830) could become `WakeContentGate` (≈100 lines).
  - **Manual-wake enqueue** (`enqueueManualWake`, `restorePendingWake`, lines ~357–490) could sit in a thin `WakeEnqueuer` façade.
  - **`_drain` / `_executeJob` / `processNext`** (lines ~849–1192) are the dispatch kernel and could form `WakeDrainEngine` (≈340 lines), leaving the outer class as a thin coordinator.
  - After the split each file would be under 500 lines, and existing tests already cover each concern independently.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (5659 lines) — the mirror of the oversized impl. Concrete split:
  - One file per logical group that already exists in the source: `wake_orchestrator_subscription_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_drain_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_throttle_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_content_gate_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_abort_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_propagated_defer_test.dart`. The shared Glados generators and scenario classes in the first ~860 lines could move to a second helpers file (e.g. `wake_orchestrator_test_scenarios.dart`) since `wake_orchestrator_test_helpers.dart` already establishes this pattern.
  - Mirror-split the single Glados routing-scenario test (lines ~932–1018) into `wake_orchestrator_subscription_test.dart`; the drain-scenario Glados test (lines ~1541–1769) into `wake_orchestrator_drain_test.dart`; etc.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart` (958 lines) — heavy Glados scenario boilerplate. The per-agent state setup helpers (`_GeneratedScheduledWakeSpec.toState`, lines ~87–130) could move to the shared `test/features/agents/test_utils.dart` so the file stays under 600 lines after the scenario classes are trimmed.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:4071–4084` — `AgentSubscription` constructor test at line 4071 checks `sub.id`, `sub.agentId`, `sub.matchEntityIds`, and `sub.predicate, isNotNull`. The `isNotNull` check for the predicate field is a near-smoke test; consider asserting the predicate's return value instead (`expect(sub.predicate!({'tok'}), isTrue)`). Low severity because the surrounding tests drive the predicate behaviour thoroughly.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:1854` — `expect(capturedEntry.startedAt, isNotNull)` is a weak assertion for `startedAt`. A stronger check would be `expect(capturedEntry.startedAt, greaterThanOrEqualTo(DateTime(2024, 3, 15)))` to also confirm the clock was used correctly.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:3454` — `expect(writes.first.nextWakeAt, isNotNull)` only asserts presence; the nearby tests in the same group do check the exact deadline value, so this is consistent but could be tightened to assert the approximate deadline (e.g. within one second of `now + throttleWindow`).

- [ ] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` — the four Glados scenarios (lines 932, 1541, 2688, 2849) each inline a full mock-stub block (≈20 lines of `when(...)` calls) that is nearly identical to the `setUp` block at line 867. After the proposed file split, these blocks should delegate to a shared helper (e.g. `_stubAllRepository(repo)`) to reduce copy-paste. The existing `restubWakeRunMethods` helper (`wake_orchestrator_test_helpers.dart:68`) already does part of this; extend it to cover `getAgentState` and `upsertEntity` as well.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` — the two `group('enqueueManualWake', ...)` blocks (lines 2415 and 4175) share the same group name. The second one (line 4175, testing "clears pending subscription jobs") duplicates a concern already tested at line 2498. If these are intentionally distinct, rename the second group; if not, merge.

- [ ] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` — Tests at lines 1497–1519 (`start replaces previous subscription when called twice`) use real async (`async` test, not `fakeAsync`) with `pumpEventQueue()`. The comment explains why (`StreamSubscription.cancel()` on broadcast streams does not resolve within `fakeAsync.flushMicrotasks`). This is a legitimate exception documented in `test/README.md` ("Exception: Tests validating real I/O"). Flag for future investigation if the Dart team changes broadcast-stream cancellation semantics, but currently acceptable.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[LOW]** `WakeOrchestrator._preRegisterSuppression` + `_isPreRegisteredSuppressed` path in `_drain`: There is no standalone Glados property for "pre-registered suppression plus confirmed suppression produce the same aggregate coverage as their union". The existing drain-scenario Glados test covers this indirectly through the `_GeneratedWakeDrainScenario` model. No additional Glados test is needed — the existing property exercises it with enough combinatorial depth.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `RunKeyFactory.actionStableId` (lib:60–72) — the JSON-canonicalization invariant (key-order-independent hash) is tested by a single Glados property in `run_key_factory_test.dart:441`. That test already covers the "same args, different key order → same hash" and "different args → different hash" invariants with 120 runs. No gap.

- Overall: Glados coverage is extensive. The four Glados tests in `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (routing/drain/pending-restore/post-run-throttle) plus the per-component tests in `wake_throttle_coordinator_test.dart`, `wake_suppression_tracker_test.dart`, `wake_runner_test.dart`, `wake_queue_test.dart`, `run_key_factory_test.dart`, and `scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart` amount to 13 distinct Glados test invocations covering every non-trivial pure invariant. No genuine gap found.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:619–629` — the "escalate deadline when a fast-throttle match arrives during a propagated deferral" branch. The condition is `usesFastThrottle && deadline != null && immediate.isBefore(deadline)`. The existing test at line 3292 ("escalates deadline from morning to fast-throttle on direct match") covers the happy path. Missing: a test asserting that no escalation happens when `immediate.isAfter(deadline)` (i.e., the existing deadline is already sooner than `now + throttleWindow`). This is the `!immediate.isBefore(deadline)` branch — currently covered only indirectly by the Glados throttle scenario which includes both orderings but does not assert the non-escalation in isolation.

- [ ] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:416–432` — `start()` guard: when `_notificationSub` is already non-null, it is cancelled before the new subscription is attached. The test at line 1497 (`start replaces previous subscription`) covers the "old stream is ignored" invariant but does NOT verify that the old subscription was actually cancelled (no `verify` on the old controller's listener count). A targeted assertion via `expect(controller1.hasListener, isFalse)` after the second `start` call would close this gap.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:981–1014` — `_executeJob` path where `insertWakeRun` throws: covered by the test at line 2190 ("catches insertWakeRun failure, releases lock, and continues drain"). That test verifies the drain continues but does NOT assert that the runner lock was released for the failing agent; it only calls `runner.isRunning('agent-1')` and `runner.isRunning('agent-2')` — both of which are already checked at line 2218. Adequate; no gap.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `WakeRunner.runningAgentIds` stream (`wake_runner.dart:24`) — the stream emitting the current running set after each acquire/release is not tested. The Glados scenario in `wake_runner_test.dart` tests `isRunning`, `tryAcquire`, and `release` state invariants but never subscribes to `runningAgentIds`. An example-based test asserting that the stream emits the correct set after acquire and again after release would improve observability coverage.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `WakeRunner.activeStartedAtById` view (`wake_runner.dart:104`) — `startedAt(agentId)` is tested implicitly through the abort/timeout tests in `wake_orchestrator_test.dart`, but `activeStartedAtById` (the unmodifiable map view) has no direct test. It is a trivial accessor backed by `UnmodifiableMapView`, so the risk is low.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `WakeThrottleCoordinator._clearPersistedThrottle` double-check guards (`wake_throttle_coordinator.dart:141–145`) — two `if (_throttleDeadlines.containsKey(agentId)) return` guards prevent a stale async write from clobbering a newly-set deadline. The Glados persistence scenario covers the "setDeadline then clear" interleaving but does not drive the precise race where a second `setDeadline` fires between the two guards. The risk is low because the guards are defensive, but an explicit fakeAsync test driving `clear + setDeadline + flushMicrotasks` in rapid succession would pin the invariant.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator_test.dart:934,1543,2690,2851` — Four Glados properties (`numRuns: 240`, `numRuns: 220`, `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 120`), totalling 760 Glados iterations in one file. Each iteration runs through the full `setUp` block at line 867 which wires ~15 `when(...)` stubs on `MockWakeRunner`, `MockWakeThrottleCoordinator`, `MockAgentRepository`, and others. Moving the mock construction to `setUpAll` and calling `reset(mock)` (or re-applying only the stubs that change per iteration) before each Glados run would eliminate ~755 redundant mock constructions — the biggest single speed win in the wake directory.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart:266,375` — Two Glados properties (`numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 160`). The quality section notes heavy Glados boilerplate. The per-iteration `setUp` rebuilds a `ScheduledWakeManager` with all stubs each time. Moving the manager and mock construction to `setUpAll` with a lightweight `reset(mock)` between iterations would cut 335 mock-build cycles.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_throttle_coordinator_test.dart:502,622` — Two Glados properties (`numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180`). These test a pure state-machine (`WakeThrottleCoordinator`) backed by a `MockAgentRepository`. The coordinator is cheap to construct; the I/O stub cost per iteration is modest but still repeated 360 times. Shared `setUpAll` + `reset` would halve the number of coordinator instantiations.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_queue_test.dart:409` — Glados property at `numRuns: 160`. `WakeQueue` is a pure in-memory FIFO with no mocks; construction is trivial. The Glados loop here is already as fast as possible. No change needed.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/wake/run_key_factory_test.dart:443`, `wake_runner_test.dart:100`, `wake_suppression_tracker_test.dart:154` — Glados properties at `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180` respectively. All three test pure data structures with no I/O or mock overhead; they are fast by design. No fake-time violations found anywhere in the wake suite.

---

## Summary

- `wake_orchestrator.dart` (1225 lines) is the **only oversized impl file** in this subdir; five other impl files are all comfortably under 200 lines.
- `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (5659 lines) is the **only oversized test file** — it is the largest single test file in scope and needs a mirror-split into at least 5–6 smaller files aligned with its existing `group(...)` structure.
- No fake-time violations (`Future.delayed`, `DateTime.now()`, `sleep`) found anywhere in the test suite.
- No inline mock class definitions — all mocks correctly imported from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.
- Glados coverage is thorough (13 Glados invocations across 7 test files); no genuine property-test gap identified.
- **Biggest opportunity:** split `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` into mirror-aligned files — this is a maintenance burden today and a merge-conflict hot-spot on every future orchestrator change.
